message:
fix(lab): zero-range delay and wakeup storm configs treated as effectively disabled

ChaosConfig with delay_probability > 0 but a zero-width delay range
(e.g. Duration::ZERO..Duration::ZERO) would report is_enabled()=true
and attempt injection, producing only zero-duration delays that waste
cycles without injecting real chaos.  Similarly, wakeup storms with
count range 0..1 would inject 0-count storms.

Fix: Two range predicates — delay_range_can_emit_nonzero() and
wakeup_range_can_emit_positive() — gate both is_enabled(), summary(),
should_delay(), and should_wakeup_storm().  When the range cannot
produce a meaningful value, the feature is treated as disabled
regardless of its probability setting.

The delay/wakeup random value generators are also hardened: when
the range starts at 0 but the end is >1, the minimum is clamped
to 1 so the injected value is always nonzero (zero delays and
zero storms are no-ops by definition).

Tests:
- delay_probability_without_nonzero_delay_range_is_effectively_disabled
- wakeup_probability_without_positive_count_is_effectively_disabled

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
